sentence: قَالَ الرَّجُلُ الْحَكِيمُ إِنَّ الصِّدْقَ مَنْجَاةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ.
raw: قال الرجل الحكيم إن الصدق منجاة للمؤمنين.
ipa: ˈqaːla rˈradʒulu lħaˈkiːmu ˈʔinna sˤˈsˤidqa manˈdʒaːtun lilmuʔmiˈniːna
gloss_en: The wise man said that truthfulness is a salvation for the believers.
notes: uvular /q/ (watson2002 §1); sun assimilation ar-r, as-s (ryding-conventions per holes2004); geminate نّ in إِنَّ

sentence: ذَلِكَ الضَّيْفُ الْكَرِيمُ شَرِبَ الْقَهْوَةَ ثُمَّ غَادَرَ الْمَجْلِسَ.
raw: ذلك الضيف الكريم شرب القهوة ثم غادر المجلس.
ipa: ˈðalika ɮˤˈɮˤajfu lkaˈriːmu ˈʃariba lˈqahwata ˈθumma ˈɣaːdara lˈmadʒlisa
gloss_en: That noble guest drank the coffee and then left the gathering.
notes: ض = [ɮˤɮˤ] lateral-emphatic geminate after article (Sibawayhi's value, versteegh2001 ch.2; arb spec note); interdental ذ; ta marbuta before a vowel now correctly [at] (spec fix: positional pair as in ar; Wright I §297) — earlier snapshot dropped the /t/

sentence: هَلْ سَمِعْتُمْ ظَهِيرَةَ الْيَوْمِ صَوْتَ الرَّعْدِ فِي السَّحَابِ؟
raw: هل سمعتم ظهيرة اليوم صوت الرعد في السحاب؟
ipa: ˈhal saˈmiʕtum ðˤaˈhiːrata lˈjawmi ˈsˤawta rˈraʕdi ˈfiː ssaˈħaːbi
gloss_en: Did you hear, at today's midday, the sound of thunder in the clouds?
notes: emphatic interdental ظ /ðˤ/ (watson2002); diphthongs /aw/ in صَوْت /aj/ absent — يَوْم aw; wasl elision fiː s-saḥāb; ta marbuta before a vowel now correctly [at] (spec fix: positional pair as in ar; Wright I §297) — earlier snapshot dropped the /t/

sentence: لَنْ يَخْسَرَ الصَّابِرُونَ أَجْرَهُمْ أَبَدًا وَلَوْ طَالَ الزَّمَانُ.
raw: لن يخسر الصابرون أجرهم أبدا ولو طال الزمان.
ipa: ˈlan ˈjaxsara sˤsˤaːbiˈruːna ˈʔadʒrahum ˈʔabadan ˈwalaw ˈtˤaːla zzaˈmaːnu
gloss_en: The patient will never lose their reward, even if time grows long.
notes: future negation لَنْ + subjunctive (classical syntax, versteegh2001); tanwin fath + silent alif in أَبَدًا

sentence: اِشْتَرَتْ خَدِيجَةُ سَبْعَةَ أَثْوَابٍ بِالدِّينَارِ الذَّهَبِيِّ.
raw: اشترت خديجة سبعة أثواب بالدينار الذهبي.
ipa: ˈʔiʃtarat xaˈdiːdʒatu ˈsabʕata ʔaθˈwaːbin biddiːˈnaːri ððahaˈbijji
gloss_en: Khadija bought seven garments with the golden dinar.
notes: wasl-initial verb اِشْتَرَتْ; ta marbuta liaison /tu/ in خَدِيجَةُ; interdentals ث ذ; geminate يّ in الذَّهَبِيِّ; ta marbuta before a vowel now correctly [at] (spec fix: positional pair as in ar; Wright I §297) — earlier snapshot dropped the /t/

sentence: خُذْ كِتَابَكَ وَقُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ.
raw: خذ كتابك وقم إلى الصلاة.
ipa: ˈxuð kiˈtaːbaka ˈwaqum ˈʔilaː sˤsˤaˈlaːti
gloss_en: Take your book and rise to prayer.
notes: Classical imperatives xudh/qum with full i'rab retained; sun assimilation as-salati

sentence: لَيْسَ الْجَوُّ حَارًّا الْيَوْمَ وَالسَّمَاءُ غَائِمَةٌ.
raw: ليس الجو حارا اليوم والسماء غائمة.
ipa: ˈlajsa lˈdʒawwu ˈħaːrran alˈjawma wassaˈmaːʔu ɣaːˈʔimatun
gloss_en: The weather is not hot today and the sky is cloudy.
notes: laysa negation; ta marbuta [t] before the case vowel (versteegh2001)

sentence: بِكَمِ الثَّوْبُ؟ بِخَمْسَةِ دَنَانِيرَ وَنِصْفٍ.
raw: بكم الثوب؟ بخمسة دنانير ونصف.
ipa: ˈbikami θˈθawbu biˈxamsati danaːˈniːra waˈnisˤfin
gloss_en: How much is the garment? Five and a half dinars.
notes: interdental th in ath-thawbu; diptote danaaniira; ta marbuta liaison in khamsati (versteegh2001)

sentence: اَلْمَوْعِدُ فِي السَّاعَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ.
raw: الموعد في الساعة الثالثة بعد الظهر.
ipa: alˈmawʕidu ˈfiː sˈsaːʕati θθaːˈliθati ˈbaʕda ðˤˈðˤuhri
gloss_en: The appointment is at three in the afternoon.
notes: sun-assimilation chain; Classical dh in adh-dhuhri; double ta marbuta liaison (watson2002)

sentence: أَخِي الْأَكْبَرُ يَسْكُنُ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ مَعَ زَوْجَتِهِ وَأَوْلَادِهِ.
raw: أخي الأكبر يسكن في المدينة مع زوجته وأولاده.
ipa: ˈʔaxiː lˈʔakbaru ˈjaskunu ˈfiː lmaˈdiːnati ˈmaʕa zawˈdʒatihi waʔawˈlaːdihi
gloss_en: My eldest brother lives in the city with his wife and children.
notes: article elision after akhii; possessive chains (versteegh2001)

sentence: سِرْ قُدُمًا ثُمَّ مِلْ يَمِينًا عِنْدَ الْمَسْجِدِ.
raw: سر قدما ثم مل يمينا عند المسجد.
ipa: ˈsir ˈquduman ˈθumma ˈmil jaˈmiːnan ˈʕinda lˈmasdʒidi
gloss_en: Go forward, then incline right at the mosque.
notes: Classical imperatives sir/mil; adverbial tanwin pair

sentence: كَانَ الطَّعَامُ طَيِّبًا لَكِنَّ الْمَرَقَةَ كَانَتْ بَارِدَةً.
raw: كان الطعام طيبا لكن المرقة كانت باردة.
ipa: ˈkaːna tˤtˤaˈʕaːmu ˈtˤajjiban laˈkinna lmaˈraqata ˈkaːnat baːˈridatan
gloss_en: The food was good but the broth was cold.
notes: kaana + accusative; maraqata shows ta marbuta [t] before the accusative vowel (versteegh2001)

sentence: أَيْنَ وَضَعْتَ الْمِفْتَاحَ؟ لَمْ أَجِدْهُ.
raw: أين وضعت المفتاح؟ لم أجده.
ipa: ˈʔajna waˈɮˤaʕta lmifˈtaːħa ˈlam ʔaˈdʒidhu
gloss_en: Where did you put the key? I did not find it.
notes: lam + jussive; Sibawayhi lateral dad in wada'ta (versteegh2001)

sentence: كَمْ رَجُلًا جَاءَ أَمْسِ؟ جَاءَ سَبْعَةٌ.
raw: كم رجلا جاء أمس؟ جاء سبعة.
ipa: ˈkam ˈradʒulan ˈdʒaːʔa ˈʔamsi ˈdʒaːʔa ˈsabʕatun
gloss_en: How many men came yesterday? Seven came.
notes: kam + accusative; medial+final hamza (versteegh2001)

sentence: إِذَا جِئْتَ مُبَكِّرًا ذَهَبْنَا مَعًا.
raw: إذا جئت مبكرا ذهبنا معا.
ipa: ˈʔiðaː ˈdʒiʔta muˈbakkiran ðaˈhabnaː ˈmaʕan
gloss_en: If you come early we go together.
notes: idhaa conditional; hamza on yaa seat (versteegh2001)

sentence: ذَهَبْتُ أَمْسِ إِلَى السُّوقِ وَأَخَذْتُ سَمَكًا.
raw: ذهبت أمس إلى السوق وأخذت سمكا.
ipa: ðaˈhabtu ˈʔamsi ˈʔilaː sˈsuːqi waʔaˈxaðtu ˈsamakan
gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the market and took fish.
notes: past narrative; interdental dh; wa+hamzat qat' retained (versteegh2001)

sentence: غَدًا نُسَافِرُ فِي الصَّبَاحِ الْبَاكِرِ.
raw: غدا نسافر في الصباح الباكر.
ipa: ˈɣadan nuˈsaːfiru ˈfiː sˤsˤaˈbaːħi lˈbaːkiri
gloss_en: Tomorrow we travel in the early morning.
notes: ghadan future-time; emphatic s backing (watson2002)

sentence: قَالَ الْحَكِيمُ إِنَّ الْعِلْمَ نُورٌ وَالْجَهْلَ ظَلَامٌ.
raw: قال الحكيم إن العلم نور والجهل ظلام.
ipa: ˈqaːla lħaˈkiːmu ˈʔinna lˈʕilma ˈnuːrun walˈdʒahla ðˤaˈlaːmun
gloss_en: The sage said knowledge is light and ignorance darkness.
notes: qaala + inna accusative pair; Classical zh [dh-emphatic] in dhalaam (watson2002)

sentence: صَبَاحُ الْخَيْرِ، كَيْفَ حَالُكَ الْيَوْمَ؟ بِخَيْرٍ، شُكْرًا.
raw: صباح الخير، كيف حالك اليوم؟ بخير، شكرا.
ipa: sˤaˈbaːħu lˈxajri ˈkajfa ˈħaːluka lˈjawma biˈxajrin ˈʃukran
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you today? Well, thanks.
notes: greeting adjacency pair; tanwin -in pre-pausal (versteegh2001)

sentence: اَلصَّبْرُ مِفْتَاحُ الْفَرَجِ.
raw: الصبر مفتاح الفرج.
ipa: asˤˈsˤabru mifˈtaːħu lˈfaradʒi
gloss_en: Patience is the key to relief.
notes: proverb; idafa with article elision (versteegh2001)
